skill.name: plan-generator
skill.description: Automatically generates a Markdown final-exam review plan or lab experiment schedule when you provide a date range, tasks/items, and available daily hours (via interactive prompts or a one-time JSON input).
skill.summary: Creates day-by-day Markdown study plans or lab schedules from your tasks, dates, and available hours.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 50 static analysis findings are false positives after manual evaluation. The static scanner misinterpreted Markdown fenced code blocks in SKILL.md as shell backtick execution (34 findings), and flagged generic text keywords as weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and certificate patterns. The actual Python scripts (plan_generator.py, validate_skill.py) are safe local-only tools that read user input and write Markdown files with no network access, credential handling, or dangerous operations. The external_commands risk factor is valid because the skill documents executing Python scripts, but this is the intended and documented usage pattern with no injection risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks mistaken for shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 34 occurrences of backtick-enclosed text in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are standard Markdown fenced code blocks showing example bash commands in documentation. No actual command injection or dynamic execution is present. The commands (python scripts/plan_generator.py) are benign documentation examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are Markdown fenced code blocks containing example bash commands. The pattern is well-known as a false positive in static analysis of documentation files.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm false positives
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 7 locations as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' in non-cryptographic contexts. Flagged locations include JSON audit note fields, an argparse description string, and markdown documentation text. No cryptographic operations exist anywhere in this codebase.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic functions, imports, or algorithms exist in any file. The scanner matched keyword patterns in unrelated text content such as JSON notes and code descriptions.

low_findings.title: System and network reconnaissance false positives
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 3 locations as reconnaissance patterns based on keyword matching. These include a comment about topological sort fallback logic in plan_generator.py, and text strings in the audit result JSON. The skill explicitly states 'No network access' at SKILL.md:38 and performs no system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Flagged locations are either benign comments, JSON text fields, or the explicit statement that the skill has no network access. No reconnaissance behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Certificate/key file false positive
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged line 180 of plan_generator.py as 'Certificate/key files'. The line contains a Python dictionary comprehension using the variable name 'key' as in dictionary key lookup. No SSL/TLS certificates, cryptographic keys, or credential files are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a dictionary comprehension using item['name'] as keys for a topological sort lookup. The scanner matched on the word 'key' in a variable context unrelated to security credentials.

content.user_title: Generate study plans and lab schedules from your tasks
content.value_statement: Manually organizing exam reviews or lab experiments into a daily schedule is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill creates a day-by-day Markdown plan from your tasks, dates, and available hours.
actual_capabilities: Create a review plan for final exams by specifying course names, exam dates, and priority levels.
Generate lab experiment schedules with task durations, dependencies, and resource requirements.
Support both interactive input mode and one-time JSON file input mode.
Produce day-by-day Markdown schedules that account for different weekday and weekend hours.
Automatically order lab tasks based on dependency constraints using topological sorting.
Export the generated plan as a Markdown file to the current working directory.
limitations: Does not integrate with calendar applications or send reminders.
Cannot import tasks from external sources like learning management systems.
Does not support task prioritization beyond a simple importance ranking.
Assumes fixed daily hours and does not adapt to schedule changes dynamically.

use_cases.title: Final exam review planning
use_cases.description: A university student has multiple final exams across two weeks and needs an optimized daily study schedule.

use_cases.title: Lab experiment scheduling
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to sequence lab experiments with equipment dependencies and time constraints.

use_cases.title: Course material review
use_cases.description: A learner wants to distribute review time across several subjects before certification exams.

prompt_templates.title: Basic review plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a review plan from 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-14. I have 3 hours on weekdays and 5 hours on weekends. My courses are: Linear Algebra (exam June 15, high importance), Operating Systems (exam June 18, medium importance), and Database Systems (exam June 20, high importance).
prompt_templates.scenario: A student needs a simple exam review schedule across two weeks.

prompt_templates.title: Lab schedule with dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a lab schedule from 2026-03-01 to 2026-03-07. I have 6 hours on weekdays and 4 hours on weekends. Tasks: Experiment A (6 hours, needs Centrifuge), Experiment B (4 hours, depends on A, needs PCR Machine), Analysis (3 hours, depends on B).
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to sequence lab tasks with resource constraints and prerequisites.

prompt_templates.title: JSON input mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the plan generator in JSON mode using the input file at input_review.json, then show me the generated Markdown output.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has prepared structured input data in a JSON file for reproducible scheduling.

prompt_templates.title: Custom study plan with topics
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 1-week review plan starting 2026-07-01 with 4 hours daily. Subjects: Physics (topics: Mechanics, Thermodynamics, Optics), Chemistry (topics: Organic, Inorganic), Biology (topics: Cell Biology, Genetics).
prompt_templates.scenario: A student wants to specify detailed topics to distribute across study sessions.

output_examples.input: Create a review plan for 2 weeks with Linear Algebra and Operating Systems.
output: - Daily available time: working day 3 Hour, weekend 5 Hour
- Linear Algebra | take an exam: 2026-06-15 | theme: Vectors, Matrices, Eigenvalues
- Operating Systems | take an exam: 2026-06-18 | theme: Processes, Scheduling, Memory

output_examples.input: Create a lab schedule for 3 experiments with dependencies.
output: - Daily available time: working day 6 Hour, weekend 4 Hour
- Experiment A | duration: 6h | rely: none | resource: Centrifuge
- Experiment B | duration: 4h | rely: Experiment A | resource: PCR Machine
- Analysis | duration: 3h | rely: Experiment B | resource: none

best_practices: Provide accurate daily hour estimates including separate weekday and weekend values for realistic schedules.
List all task dependencies clearly so the topological sorter can create a valid execution order.
Use the one-time JSON input mode for reproducible plan generation across multiple runs.
anti_patterns: Do not omit task duration estimates in lab mode as the scheduler cannot allocate time without them.
Do not set unrealistically high daily hours as this produces schedules that cannot be followed.
Do not use overlapping or contradictory date ranges where end date is before start date.

faq.question: What input formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports interactive prompts and one-time JSON file input. Use the --json flag to load a JSON file with your schedule data.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-academic planning?
faq.answer: Yes. The review plan type works for any set of topics or tasks, and the lab mode works for any project with dependencies.

faq.question: Does this skill access the internet?
faq.answer: No. The skill runs entirely on your local machine. It reads a local JSON file if provided and writes the output to your current directory.

faq.question: What file does the output go to?
faq.answer: By default the output is saved as plan_review.md or plan_lab.md in the current directory. You can specify a custom path with the --output flag.

faq.question: Can I schedule recurring tasks?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates a single plan for a fixed date range. It does not support recurring or repeating tasks.

faq.question: How are task dependencies handled?
faq.answer: In lab mode, the topological sorter orders tasks so that no task is scheduled before its prerequisites. Tasks with circular dependencies are placed at the end.
